front: Location: co-designed with Calvert Vaux using the Greensward Plan; contains Bethesda Fountain, the Sheep Meadow, the Bow Bridge, and the Great Lawn
Location: setting of Edward Albee's play The Zoo Story
Location: home to an obelisk known as Cleopatra's Needle
Location: site of Christo and Jeanne-Claude's 2005 installation The Gates — 7,503 bright orange gates along park paths
Location: Diane Arbus photographed an exasperated boy holding a toy hand grenade here
Location: Charles Ives wrote a composition about hearing nature sounds on a bench here 'In the Dark'
Location: Robert Smithson's 'Dialectical Landscape' article centers on its designer and reveres the Greensward Plan
Location: Ebert Ludovicus Viele was the original engineer-in-chief before Olmsted and Vaux won the design competition
Location: Ai Weiwei's 2017 Gilded Cage protest was installed here
Location: chain of parks and parkways in Boston linking several green spaces; called the Emerald Necklace
Location: designed the grounds including the Midway Plaisance and a Wooded Island for the 1893 World's Columbian Exposition in Chicago
back: 1893 World's Columbian Exposition / Midway Plaisance (Frederick Law Olmsted)

front: Location: originally wanted a canal running through the Midway Plaisance at the 1893 World's Columbian Exposition
back: 1893 World's Columbian Exposition / Midway Plaisance (Frederick Law Olmsted)

front: Architect: the McMillan Plan brought the City Beautiful movement and him to Washington D.C., following the 1893 World's Columbian Exposition

back: 1893 World's Columbian Exposition / Midway Plaisance (Frederick Law Olmsted)

front: Location: designed landscape for this North Carolina estate built by Richard Morris Hunt for George Vanderbilt
Location: his care for the surrounding forests here helped lay the groundwork for American forestry; Carl A. Schenck ran the nation's first forest school on these grounds
Location: Prospect Park in Brooklyn — co-designed with Calvert Vaux
Location: designed the campus of Stanford University and the U.S. Capitol Grounds
Location: designed the grounds of the Buffalo State Hospital with H.H. Richardson and the Buffalo park system
Location: the Olmsted Brothers firm designed a namesake Metroparks System surrounding Cleveland
Architect: his sons collaborated with the Bartholomew firm on a never-implemented park plan for Los Angeles
Architect: mentor Andrew Jackson Downing — who died in a steamboat explosion on the Hudson River — introduced him to his partner Calvert Vaux
Architect: his mentor Andrew Jackson Downing was the first American exponent of landscape architecture
Architect: authored The Cotton Kingdom (Journeys and Explorations in the Cotton Kingdom) — argued slavery made the antebellum South economically inefficient
Architect: The Cotton Kingdom was commissioned after the success of his earlier travelogue Walks and Talks of an American Farmer in England
Architect: The Cotton Kingdom is discussed in Chapter 6 of Edmund Wilson's Patriotic Gore
Architect: his seven design S's — Scenery, Suitability, Style, Subordination, Separation, Sanitation, and Service
Architect: first executive secretary of the United States Sanitary Commission — a Civil War relief organization led by Henry Whitney Bellows
Architect: John Singer Sargent painted a portrait of him holding a walking stick in the woods
Architect: near the end of his life, ironically committed to McLean Hospital in Waverly, Massachusetts — whose grounds he had designed
Architect: co-founder of The Nation magazine
Architect: chairman of the first Yosemite Commission
Architect: before his design career, was a merchant, apprentice seaman, experimental farmer, and goldmine manager
